metadata.summary: File based cache for resources and metadata

documentation:
[![Project generated with PyScaffold](https://img.shields.io/badge/-PyScaffold-005CA0?logo=pyscaffold)](https://pyscaffold.org/)
[![PyPI-Server](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pyBiocFileCache.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/pyBiocFileCache/)
![Unit tests](https://github.com/BiocPy/pyBiocFileCache/actions/workflows/pypi-test.yml/badge.svg)

# pyBiocFileCache

File system based cache for resources & metadata. Compatible with [BiocFileCache R package](https://github.com/Bioconductor/BiocFileCache)

***Note: Package is in development. Use with caution!!***

### Installation

Package is published to [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/pyBiocFileCache/)

```
pip install pybiocfilecache
```

#### Initialize a cache directory

```
from pybiocfilecache import BiocFileCache
import os

bfc = BiocFileCache(cache_dir = os.getcwd() + "/cache")
```

Once the cache directory is created, the library provides methods to
- `add`: Add a resource or artifact to cache
- `get`: Get the resource from cache
- `remove`: Remove a resource from cache
- `update`: update the resource in cache
- `purge`: purge the entire cache, removes all files in the cache directory

### Add a resource to cache

(for testing use the temp files in the `tests/data` directory)

```
rec = bfc.add("test1", os.getcwd() + "/test1.txt")
print(rec)
```

### Get resource from cache

```
rec = bfc.get("test1")
print(rec)
```

### Remove resource from cache

```
rec = bfc.remove("test1")
print(rec)
```

### Update resource in cache

```
rec = bfc.get("test1"m os.getcwd() + "test2.txt")
print(rec)
```

### purge the cache

```
bfc.purge()
```


<!-- pyscaffold-notes -->

## Note

This project has been set up using PyScaffold 4.1. For details and usage

functions.name: pybiocfilecache.utils.create_tmp_dir
documentation:
Create a temporary directory.

Returns:
    Temporary path to the directory.

functions.name: pybiocfilecache.utils.generate_id
documentation:
Generate uuid.

Returns:
    Unique string for use as id.

functions.name: pybiocfilecache.utils.copy_or_move
documentation:
Copy or move a resource from `source` to `target`.

Args:
: source:

  : Source location of the resource to copy of move.

  target:

  : Destination to copy of move to.

  rname:

  : Name of resource to add to cache.

  action:

  : Copy of move file from source.
    Defaults to copy.

Raises:
: ValueError:

  : If action is not `copy`, `move` or `asis`.

  Exception:

  : Error storing resource in the cache directory.

functions.name: pybiocfilecache.utils.setup_logging
documentation:
Setup basic logging.

Args:
  loglevel (int): minimum loglevel for emitting messages

functions.name: pybiocfilecache.db.db_config.create_schema
documentation:
Create the schema in the sqlite database.

Args:
    cache_dir:
        Location where the cache directory.

Returns:
    A tuple of sqlalchemy engine and session maker.

classes.name: pybiocfilecache._exceptions.NoFpathError
documentation: An error for when the source file does not exist.

classes.name: pybiocfilecache._exceptions.RnameExistsError
documentation: An error for when the key already exists in the cache.

classes.name: pybiocfilecache._exceptions.RpathTimeoutError
documentation: An error for when the 'rpath' does not exist after a timeout.

classes.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache

methods.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache.__init__
documentation:
Initialize BiocFileCache.

Args:
: cacheDirOrPath:

  : Path to cache directory.

    Defaults to tmp location, {py:func}`~.utils.create_tmp_dir`.

Raises:

: Exception: Failed to initialize cache.

methods.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache.add
documentation:
Add a resource from the provided `fpath` to cache as `rname`.

Args:
: rname:

  : Name of the resource to add to cache.

  fpath:

  : Location of the resource.

  rtype:

  : One of `local`, `web`, or `relative`.
    Defaults to `local`.

  action:

  : Either `copy`, `move` or `asis`.
    Defaults to `copy`.

  ext:

  : Whether to use filepath extension when storing in cache.
    Defaults to `False`.

Raises:
: NoFpathError:

  : When the `fpath` does not exist.

  RnameExistsError:

  : When the `rname` already exists in the cache.
    sqlalchemy exceptions: When something is up with the cache.

Returns:

: Database record of the new resource in cache.

methods.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache.query
documentation:
Search cache for a resource.

Args:
    query:
        Query string or keywords to search.

    field:
        Field to search.
        Defaults to "rname".

Returns:
    List of matching resources from cache.

methods.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache._get
documentation:
Get a resource with `rname` from given `Session`.

Args:
    session:
        The `Session` object to use.

    rname:
        The `rname` of the `Resource` to get.

Returns:
    The `Resource` for the `rname` if available.

methods.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache.get
documentation:
Get resource by name from cache.

Args:
    rname:
        Name of the file to search.

Returns:
    Matched `Resource` from cache if exists.

methods.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache.remove
documentation:
Remove a resource from cache by name.

Args:
    rname:
        Name of the resource to remove.

methods.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache.purge
documentation: Remove all files from cache.

methods.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache.update
documentation:
Update a resource in cache.

Args:
: rname:

  : Name of the resource in cache.

  fpath:

  : New resource to replace existing file in cache.

  action:

  : Either `copy`, `move` or `asis`.

    Defaults to `copy`.

Returns:

: Updated resource record in cache.

methods.name: pybiocfilecache.BiocFileCache.BiocFileCache.list_all
documentation:
List all resources currently in the cache.

Returns:
    List of all Resource objects in the cache.
Class to manage and cache files.
